visual_notes: Unmistakable greasy feel and extremely dark matte-black color. Marks paper and skin readily. Steel-gray metallic luster on fresh cleavage faces. Often found as disseminated flakes or foliated masses in metamorphic rocks.
prompt_fragments: foliated dark graphite plates with greasy metallic sheen
matte black scaly graphite in white marble matrix
carbon's humble terrestrial form — dark and slippery
vibe_notes: Graphite is diamond's humble alter ego — same element, opposite outcome. Where diamond captures and refracts, graphite absorbs and slides. It is carbon in its most pragmatic, terrestrial form.
folklore_summary: Graphite was initially called 'plumbago' (black lead) when discovered in Borrowdale in the 1560s — mistaken for a form of lead.
